---
title: "Lecture 02 Notes: Actuation I - Electric Motors, Transmissions, and Torque Generation"
---

## Lecture slide

- [Lecture 02 slides](../slides/13-actuation-1-motors-transmissions-torque.qmd)

## Key ideas

- Actuators convert electrical power into controllable mechanical motion.
- Transmission choices trade output speed, torque, and backdrivability.
- Continuous torque and thermal limits often determine usable performance.

## Core equations

- Motor torque: \(\tau_m = K_t i\)
- Back EMF: \(e = K_e \omega_m\)
- With gear ratio \(N\): \(\tau_{out} \approx \eta N \tau_m\), \(\omega_{out} = \omega_m / N\)

## Review questions

1. Why does a higher gear ratio increase output torque but reduce speed?
2. How do efficiency losses change actuator sizing decisions?
3. Why is thermal behavior critical for repeated robotic tasks?
